dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3831 IS: Enhancing Multi-Class Share Disclosures Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Gallego (for himself and Mr. Rounds) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To amend the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require issuers with a multi-class stock structure to make certain disclosures in any proxy or consent solicitation material, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Enhancing Multi-Class Share Disclosures Act.

section.header: Disclosure relating to multi-class share structures
section.text: Section 14 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (15 U.S.C. 78n) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Disclosure for issuers with multi-Class share structures

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the term multi-class share structure means a capitalization structure that contains 2 or more classes of securities that have differing amounts of voting rights in the election of directors.
The Commission shall, by rule, require each issuer with a multi-class share structure to disclose the information described in paragraph (3) in—

subparagraph.text: any proxy or consent solicitation material for an annual meeting of the shareholders of the issuer; or
any other filing, as the Commission determines appropriate.

paragraph.text: A disclosure made under paragraph (2) shall include, with respect to each person who is a director, director nominee, or named executive officer of the issuer, or who is a beneficial owner of securities with 5 percent or more of the total combined voting power of all classes of securities entitled to vote in the election of directors of the issuer—

subparagraph.text: the number of shares of all classes of securities entitled to vote in the election of directors beneficially owned by that person, expressed as a percentage of the total number of the outstanding securities of the issuer entitled to vote in the election of directors; and

the amount of voting power held by that person, expressed as a percentage of the total combined voting power of all classes of the securities of the issuer entitled to vote in the election of directors.
